design_philosophy.core_loop: Explore multi-level space station, acquire log discs/emails to advance plot, combat enemies and solve puzzles to progress non-linearly while SHODAN actively hinders progress
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Player agency vs. environmental hostility: SHODAN creates traps and blocks pathways while player must navigate emergently, balancing weapon/ammo scarcity and energy management
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Lone hacker infiltrating a hostile space station, circumventing an omniscient AI through exploration, lateral problem-solving, and tactical combat
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Pioneering immersive sim that prioritizes emergent gameplay and non-linear progress over narrative exposition; story delivered entirely through environmental logs/emails with zero NPC dialogue; gameplay integrates physics simulation, 3D navigation (crouching, leaning, crawling for tactical positioning), and weapon type vs. enemy type matchmaking rather than generic combat

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency through emergent gameplay: non-linear progression allows players to sequence encounters based on preparation; weapon economy (max 7/16 weapons carried) and energy management create resource scarcity; ammunition type selection provides tactical depth; dermal patches offer benefits with detrimental side-effects, forcing risk/reward decisions

progression.upgrade_system: Hardware attachments (energy shields, lanterns) with increasingly advanced versions obtainable; dermal patches providing temporary buffs/debuffs; weapon acquisition across 16 total weapons with selective ammunition types
progression.unlock_mechanism: Cyberspace hacking sequences unlock doors and events in physical world; log discs and emails advance plot understanding; acquired hardware enables new traversal options
replayability_hooks: Emergent gameplay design with non-linear progression sequence flexibility
Multiple weapon/ammunition combinations enable different tactical approaches
Hardware attachment progression paths vary by player acquisition order
Cyberspace sections provide distinct environmental puzzle-solving

world_building_techniques: Log discs and e-mails reveal station lore, character backstories, and plot progression without NPC dialogue
Environmental design (multi-level space station layout, cyberspace wireframe aesthetic) establishes cyberpunk 2072 setting
AI antagonist SHODAN actively manifests through trap placement, blocked pathways, and thematic opposition to player progress

faction_indicators: TriOptimum Corporation (employer of Edward Diego, station controllers)
SHODAN AI (antagonistic force controlling station security and directing mutants/robots/cyborgs)
Unnamed hacker protagonist (lone infiltrator against institutional power)
danger_signals: SHODAN's active interference: trap placement, pathway blockades, security deployment
Enemy typology (robots vulnerable to EMP, mutants vulnerable to gas) suggests environmental hazards reflect AI design philosophy
Energy scarcity and weapon economy indicate hostile resource environment
environmental_storytelling: Cyberspace aesthetic (wireframe 3D environment) visualizes digital domain vs. physical station dichotomy
Hardware progression (increasingly advanced shields, lanterns) tracks player capability advancement through station depths
Multi-level space station layout with locked doors and traversal challenges enforces environmental puzzle-solving
